name: La Hacienda
description: One free kids' meal per adult entrée

meta.notes: Broussard location. Available every day.

name: Bushido Sushi & Korean BBQ
description: Kids under 5 eat free. Kids 5–12 get discounted AYCE pricing.

meta.notes: Under 5 free every day. Ages 5–12: Lunch AYCE $11.99, Dinner & Weekends $16.99.

name: The French Press
description: Beloved Lafayette institution. Bright, casual, locally beloved. Nationally acclaimed breakfast/brunch.

meta.notes: Two locations: 214 E Vermilion St (Downtown) and 3822 Ambassador Caffery Pkwy. Featured in SAVEUR Magazine Top 100. Open Mon, Wed–Sun 7 AM–2 PM; Sat–Sun 9 AM–2 PM. Note: Original downtown location may have changed hours – verify.

name: Park Bistro
description: Upscale yet comfortable neighborhood bistro. Patio seating, excellent cocktails, lively ambiance.

meta.notes: 407 Brook Ave, Lafayette. Tue–Thu 5–9 PM; Fri 11–2 & 5–10 PM; Sat 5–10 PM; Sun 10–3 PM. Live music on select Wednesday nights. Reservations strongly recommended for weekend brunch.

name: Cafe Sydnie Mae
description: Charming Cajun cafe in historic downtown Breaux Bridge. Live music, art, and community feel.

meta.hours: Saturday 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM / Sunday 9:45 AM – 2:00 PM
meta.notes: 140 E Bridge St, Breaux Bridge. (337) 909-2377. Live local music during brunch. Indoor and outdoor seating. Rated 4.7/5 on TripAdvisor. One of the region's most beloved brunch spots.

name: Market Eatz
description: Neighborhood full-service dining and bar. Casual, family-friendly, locally owned.

meta.notes: Lafayette location: 819 E Broussard Rd. Happy Hour M–F 3–6 PM. Brunch Sat & Sun 10:30 AM–2 PM. Two locations; fans rave about cocktails and locally sourced menu.

name: Ruby Slipper
description: Lively Southern brunch chain with Louisiana roots. Cheerful and energetic. Great for groups.
features: Eggs Cochon (pork debris over poached eggs)

meta.notes: 4701 Ambassador Caffery Pkwy, Lafayette. Mon–Fri 7:30 AM–2 PM; Weekends 7:30 AM–3 PM. Popular regional chain with strong following. Consistently rated top brunch spot on Yelp.

name: Spoonbill Watering Hole & Restaurant
description: Downtown Lafayette gem. Tells a story through food and drink. Laid-back but thoughtful.

meta.notes: Downtown Lafayette. No reservations taken. Sun 10 AM–2 PM. Tue–Sat 11 AM–9/10 PM. Rated 4.6/5 on TripAdvisor. Do not take reservations.

name: Tchoup's MidCity Restaurant + Bar
description: American smokehouse with elevated bar. Dog-friendly patio. Smoky cocktails, BBQ brunch.

meta.notes: 117 S College Rd, Lafayette. Sunday brunch 10:30 AM–3 PM. Restaurant open until 8 PM Sundays. Outdoor seating, dog-friendly.

name: Blue Dog Cafe
description: Upscale Cajun cafe home to George Rodrigue's Blue Dog art. Live music during Sunday brunch. Sophisticated Louisiana feel.

meta.notes: 1211 W Pinhook Rd, Lafayette. Sunday brunch 10 AM–2 PM with live music. Check current status – reported closed on some review sites as of early 2026; verify before visiting.

name: Social Southern Table & Bar
description: Farm-to-table approach. Fresh, creative dishes. Relaxed and social atmosphere.

meta.notes: Lafayette. Consistently ranked in Yelp's top brunch and breakfast spots. Farm-to-table approach for brunch, lunch, and dinner.

name: Scratch Farm Kitchen
description: Farm-fresh and scratch-made. Local ingredients, relaxed and welcoming.

meta.notes: 2801 Johnston St, Lafayette. Consistently rated in top 10 on Yelp for brunch/breakfast in Lafayette.

name: Sundays Soda Fountain
description: Retro soda fountain vibe in Downtown Lafayette. Live music during brunch. Unique and charming.

meta.notes: 431 Jefferson St, Lafayette (Downtown). Relatively new spot. Live music makes the ambiance perfect for a good Louisiana brunch. Check social media for current hours.

name: Buck & Johnny's – Zydeco Breakfast
description: World-famous Zydeco Breakfast! Live zydeco band, dancing, Cajun-Italian breakfast bites. One of a kind.

meta.reservations: Not needed – First Come First Served
meta.notes: 100 Berard St, Breaux Bridge. Doors open 8 AM, music 8:30–11:30 AM. No reservations – arrive early! Family-friendly. One of the most unique brunch experiences in Louisiana.

name: Prejean's Restaurant – Weekend Brunch
description: Iconic Cajun restaurant with live morning music. Big Al the gator greets you at the door.

meta.notes: 3480 NE Evangeline Thruway, Lafayette. Live Cajun music Sat–Sun mornings 9–11 AM, and every evening from 7 PM. Reservations on OpenTable.

name: Dark Roux
description: Contemporary Southern brunch showcasing Louisiana's culinary heritage. Innovative craft cocktails, vibrant and energetic.

meta.notes: Lafayette. Sat brunch 9 AM–2 PM. Known for bartender creativity and craft cocktails. Lively patio atmosphere.

name: Five Mile Eatery
description: Casual, neighborhood-style breakfast and brunch. Unpretentious and delicious.

meta.notes: 317 Heymann Blvd, Lafayette. Mon–Sat 7 AM–3 PM; closed Sunday. Budget-friendly option for a solid weekend breakfast/brunch on Saturdays.

name: Hub City Diner
description: Classic diner energy with a Louisiana twist. Comfort food, strong coffee, no-fuss atmosphere.

meta.notes: Lafayette. Local favorite for no-frills diner-style breakfast and brunch. Affordable and reliable.

name: Bon Temps Grill
description: Laid-back Louisiana grill. Good times and good food in a casual setting.

meta.notes: Lafayette. Appeared in Yelp top brunch/breakfast lists for Lafayette.

name: The Southern Spread
description: Southern comfort food with great music selection. Refreshing service and incredible food.

meta.notes: Lafayette. Mentioned in KPEL's top Acadiana brunch list. Known for 'refreshing service with great music selection.'

name: So Lou
description: Southern Louisiana-inspired dining. Local ingredients, creative menu.

meta.notes: Lafayette. Appeared in Yelp top brunch lists. Verify current hours via social media.

name: Artmosphere
description: Artist-focused bistro by day, lively live music venue by night. Covered in local artwork, large patio, full bar with specialty cocktails.

meta.notes: Live music 7 days a week. One of Acadiana's top indie music venues. Located at 902 Johnston St, Lafayette. Bottomless brunch available.

name: Bon Temps Grill
description: Upscale Cajun restaurant with nightly live music

meta.notes: Live music 7 days a week — one of the best dinner-and-music combos in Lafayette. Swamp-edge cuisine in an urban Cajun atmosphere.

name: Buck & Johnny's
description: Cajun-Italian restaurant in a restored motor building. Famous Saturday Zydeco Breakfast with a balcony overlooking the dance floor. First come, first served.

musicNights.description: World-Famous Zydeco Breakfast – live zydeco music while you dine

meta.notes: 100 Berard St, Breaux Bridge. No reservations for Zydeco Breakfast – doors open at 8 AM. Yvette Landry and Richard Comeaux have standing residency Thu–Sat evenings.

name: Cafe Sydnie Mae
description: Charming downtown Breaux Bridge café with live local music and art. Modern Cajun cuisine. Warm, community feel.

musicNights.description: Live music during brunch and evening service

meta.notes: 140 E Bridge St, Breaux Bridge. Live music on weekends. Also doubles as a brunch destination.

name: Charley G's Seafood Grill
description: Upscale seafood restaurant with live jazz

meta.notes: Fine dining seafood with live jazz several nights a week. Great for a date night with music.

name: Legends of Lafayette
description: Sports bar & grill with live bands on weekends

meta.notes: Pub atmosphere with regular live bands. Good bar food and a lively crowd.

name: O'Darby's Pub & Grill
description: Irish-style pub & grill with weekend live music

meta.notes: Neighborhood pub in Carencro with regular live music on weekends.

name: Pont Breaux's Cajun Restaurant
description: Formerly Mulate's – the original Cajun restaurant. Authentic Cajun food and live music. Known for seafood and traditional recipes.

meta.notes: 325 W Mills Ave, Breaux Bridge. (337) 332-4648. Formerly operated as Mulate's Cajun Restaurant; same tradition of nightly live music. Call ahead to verify current schedule.

name: Prejean's Restaurant
description: Beloved Cajun restaurant with dance floor and live music every night. Home to Big Al, a 14-foot stuffed alligator. Family-friendly.

meta.notes: Located at 3480 NE Evangeline Thruway (I-49 N), Lafayette. Famous for gumbo, wild game, and seafood. Reservations available via OpenTable.

name: Randol's Restaurant & Cajun Dance Hall
description: Classic Acadiana dance hall with large dance floor. Open 40+ years. Locals and tourists two-stepping nightly. Great crawfish.

musicNights.description: Live Cajun music and dancing (e.g. Maegan Benoit)

meta.notes: Located at 2320 Kaliste Saloom Rd, Lafayette. Known for crawfish by the pound. Live music nightly; call ahead to confirm specific nights.

name: Rock'n'Bowl de Lafayette
description: Unique combo of bowling alley and live music venue. Dance floor in the lanes. Restaurant and full bar. High-energy crowd.

musicNights.description: Zydeco Sunday – live zydeco starting around 5 PM

meta.notes: Check live music calendar on website for full schedule. Has hosted touring acts like Marc Broussard.

name: SHUCKS! The Louisiana Seafood House
description: Casual seafood restaurant with regular live music

meta.notes: Seafood house with live music 4+ nights a week. Great crawfish and boiled seafood.

name: Social Southern Table & Bar
description: Farm-to-table restaurant with weekend live music

meta.notes: Farm-to-table approach with brunch, lunch, and dinner. Live music on peak nights — busiest Thu–Sat evenings.

name: The Barrel of Broussard
description: Neighborhood bar & grill with weekend live music

meta.notes: Popular Broussard spot — live music on weekends plus karaoke on Wednesdays.

name: The Cajun Table
description: Authentic Cajun restaurant with live accordion evenings

meta.notes: Cajun music plays 24/7 (recorded), with live accordion artists on weekends. Crawfish specials and signature Cajun dishes.

name: Whiskey & Vine
description: New Orleans-style jazz lounge with curated cocktails and southern cuisine

meta.notes: Sommelier-led wine and whiskey program paired with nightly live jazz. Think New Orleans jazz club, right in Lafayette.

name: Angelle's Whiskey River Landing
description: Rustic dance hall on the banks of the Atchafalaya Basin levee. Stunning swamp views. Established 1982. Every Sunday afternoon.

musicNights.description: Live Cajun and zydeco music overlooking the Atchafalaya Basin

meta.notes: 1365 Henderson Levee Rd, Breaux Bridge area. Open every Sunday 3–9 PM. Started as a bar and boat launch. One of the most scenic music spots in Louisiana.

name: Blue Moon Saloon & Guesthouse
description: Historic 1900s home turned iconic music venue & boutique guesthouse. Intimate, eclectic, nationally acclaimed.

meta.notes: Voted one of the Best Bars in America by Esquire. Happy hour 5 PM with $3 Crown, $3 Jameson, $4 local beers. Check live music calendar online for full schedule.

name: El Sido's Zydeco & Blues Club
description: Legendary zydeco club operating since 1984. Has hosted Clifton Chenier, Buckwheat Zydeco, Rockin' Dopsie, Nathan & the Zydeco Cha-Chas. Authentic and historic.

meta.notes: St. Antoine & Martin Luther King Dr., Lafayette. Est. 1984. Check Facebook for current schedule.

name: Feed N Seed
description: Rustic feed-store-themed venue with antique décor. Large open dance floor for fais do-do (traditional Cajun dancing). Outdoor patio.

meta.notes: Downtown Lafayette area. Check Facebook for current schedule and events.

name: Fred's Lounge
description: World-famous Saturday morning institution. Beer and boudin at 7 AM. Live Cajun music broadcast on local radio. Legendary Acadiana experience.

musicNights.description: Live Cajun music broadcast on local radio – beer, boudin, dancing

meta.notes: 420 6th Street, Mamou. (318) 468-5411. Doors open 7 AM Saturdays, music 9 AM–noon. One of the most unique live music experiences in the South.

name: La Poussière Cajun Dancehall
description: Authentic Cajun dancehall open since 1955. Large dance floor, welcoming locals, inexpensive beer. A true cultural experience.

meta.notes: Grand Point Hwy area, Breaux Bridge. One of the oldest and most beloved Cajun dance halls in Acadiana. Very local crowd.

name: Liberty Theater
description: Renovated 1924 vaudeville house. Weekly Cajun music radio and TV show hosted in French. Dance floor open to attendees. Cultural landmark.

musicNights.description: Rendezvous des Cajuns – live Cajun music radio/TV show in French, with dance floor

meta.notes: 200 W Park Ave, Eunice. Part of the Eunice Louisiana prairie Cajun music trail alongside Savoy Music Center and Fred's Lounge.

name: Richard's Club
description: Legendary Zydeco roadhouse on Hwy 190 between Lawtell and Opelousas. Renowned for epic zydeco dances. Authentic and local.

meta.notes: Hwy 190, 3–4 miles west of Opelousas. Proprietor Kelvin Richard. (318) 543-6596. Verify schedule before visiting.

name: Savoy Music Center
description: Accordion shop and cultural institution. 40+ years of Saturday morning jams. Grassroots Cajun music community experience.
features: Free (donation of boudin or cracklins welcome)

musicNights.description: Open acoustic jam session – anyone can bring an instrument and join in

meta.cover: Free (donation of boudin or cracklins welcome)
meta.notes: 337-580-0899. Open Saturdays 9 AM–noon for 40+ years. Owned by accordion-maker Marc Savoy. A bucket-list Cajun music experience.

name: Slim's Y-Ki-Ki
description: Legendary historic zydeco dance hall on Hwy 182/167. Iconic venue that hosted Clifton Chenier, Rockin' Dopsie, and Boozoo Chavis. Currently undergoing restoration.

musicNights.description: Live zydeco (when open – verify before visiting)
Live zydeco (when open – verify before visiting)

meta.notes: Hwy 182/167, Opelousas. (318) 942-9980. NOTE: As of 2024, this venue is undergoing restoration. Call ahead or check social media to confirm current status before visiting.

name: The Grouse Room
description: Downtown Lafayette's premier nightclub and live music venue. State-of-the-art sound and lighting, built-in stage, balcony, courtyard. Craft cocktails.

meta.notes: Heart of downtown Lafayette. Features local and touring artists. Bar food available. Dueling pianos events held periodically.

name: Boudin
description: Smoked, steamed, spicy, or classic — every parish swears theirs is best.

contenders.caseFor: Consistent links with rich seasoning and that perfect snap. It's the benchmark people compare everyone else to.
Balanced spice, juicy texture, and cracklins that hit every time. A road-trip stop that's worth the detour.
Flavor-forward and a little bolder than average. If you like your boudin with attitude, this is your move.

allSpots.note: Underrated and climbing fast with locals. Great value and a no-frills vibe.

debateOfTheWeek.hook: Rice, pork, and pure South Louisiana pride — who really owns the crown this week?

seasonalRotation.note: When the heat hits, the flavor wars start.

name: Cracklins
description: Crunchy outside, melt-in-the-middle fat, and the eternal question: salty or pepper-hot?

contenders.caseFor: Golden crunch and reliable seasoning. People grab an extra bag before they even leave the parking lot.
Some call it unbeatable, others say too rich — which is exactly why this debate gets loud at every boil.
Peppery and intense in the best way. Not for the timid, but pure joy for heat lovers.

contenders.caseFor: One of the absolute best on Johnston with bold, balanced seasoning and consistent quality.
Youngsville favorite with great, juicy crawfish and a strong word-of-mouth following.
Rayne institution with serious flavor depth and loyal locals every season.

allSpots.note: Still a frequent go-to on Ridge with dependable boils.
Traditional boil style with no dusting.
